# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.145.0/http/mod.ts";

interface Config {
  modulePaths?: string[];
}

const config: Config = {};

if (import.meta.main) {
  for (const key in Deno.env.toObject()) {
    if (key.startsWith("MODULE_")) {
      const moduleName = key.replace("MODULE_", "");
      config.modulePaths?.push(moduleName);
    }
  }

  for (const param of new URLSearchParams(window.location.search)) {
    if (!config.modulePaths) {
      config.modulePaths = [];
    }
    config.modulePaths.push(param.name);
  }
}

async function loadModule(path: string): Promise<void> {
  try {
    await import(path);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(`Error loading module ${path}:`, error);
  }
}

const app = serve({ port: 8080 });

console.log("Server listening on port 8080");

for (const path of config.modulePaths || []) {
  loadModule(path).catch((error) => {
    console.error("Error loading module:", error);
  });
}

for await (const req of app) {
  const { url, method } = req;
  if (method === "GET") {
    for (const param of new URLSearchParams(url.search)) {
      loadModule(param.name).catch((error) => {
        console.error("Error loading module:", error);
      });
    }
  }

  const response = new Response(`Loaded modules: ${config.modulePaths.join(", ")}`);
  req.respond({ status: 200, body: response.body });
}
